steps.title: Apply margin of error to find the confidence interval
steps.content:
The sample estimate is 15% with a margin of error of 2%. The true percentage of red beads lies within this margin, giving us the range:

$$15\% - 2\% = 13\% \quad \text{to} \quad 15\% + 2\% = 17\%$$

steps.title: Convert percentage bounds to the actual number of red beads
steps.content:
With 10,000 total beads, multiply each percentage by 10,000:

Lower bound: $13\% \times 10,000 = 1,300$

Upper bound: $17\% \times 10,000 = 1,700$

Thus the actual number of red beads must satisfy $1,300 < r < 1,700$. This matches choice B — B is correct.
